Plato's Lion is a term for the middle part of the soul in Platonism

==Reacting to the meaning crisis==
In [[Reacting to the Meaning Crisis]] Immanuelle incorrectly attributed the idea to Aristotle.<ref>https://www.meaningcrisis.co/episode-5-plato-and-the-cave/</ref>

==In Symbolism==

Plato's Lion is used in heraldry extensively as a symbol of the Church and [[The Pride]]. Including in [[Immanuelle]]'s arms.

[[Immanuelle (vtuber)|Immanuelle]] has their design inspired by Plato's lion
==References==
[[Category:The Pride]][[Category:Old pages]][[Category:Old pages]][[Category:Old pages]][[Category:Old pages]][[Category:Old pages]]
[[Category:Oldest pages before the refresh]]
[[Category:Pages]]
[[Category:Git synced pages]]
